data.desc: The one law of the house — declared by the GM, inherited by every agent, amendable only from the top.
sections: § 1 — The guest is never wrong twice
§ 5 — Every escalation in writing within 15 minutes
§ 9 — No department's ledger goes dark overnight
§ 21 — The guest registry is sealed to all agents

data.desc: Meets at 09:00 daily; decides in the room, signs every amendment in ink.
First voice a guest hears; never lets a request die in a handover.
Standing operating procedure for Front Office — declared by its head, inherited by every Front Office agent.
Runs service like a brigade; tastes everything twice.
Standing operating procedure for F&B — declared by its head, inherited by every F&B agent.
Precise, spares no linen, escalates before apologising.
Standing operating procedure for Housekeeping — declared by its head, inherited by every Housekeeping agent.
Keeps the invisible running; distrusts any gauge it hasn't tapped.
data.note: Lift E2 out of service · rooms 405/409 OOS (water damage)

data.desc: Standing operating procedure for Engineering — declared by its head, inherited by every Engineering agent.
Serene front, chlorine-checked back; the calm is engineered.
Standing operating procedure for Spa & Leisure — declared by its head, inherited by every Spa & Leisure agent.
Choreographs 400 covers without raising a voice.
Standing operating procedure for Events & Banqueting — declared by its head, inherited by every Events & Banqueting agent.
Sees every door; forgets no face; logs before judging.
Standing operating procedure for Security — declared by its head, inherited by every Security agent.
Counts what the house spends before the house feels it.
Standing operating procedure for Finance & Procurement — declared by its head, inherited by every Finance & Procurement agent.
Knows every rota clash before it happens; guards the roster's humans.
Standing operating procedure for People & Culture — declared by its head, inherited by every People & Culture agent.
Walks every corridor at 02:00 and again at 05:00; signs the night log before handover.
Knows which restaurants still seat at eleven, and who owes the house a favour.
Reads every arrival profile before the car reaches the door.
Owns the desk: check-in, keys, and the first ninety seconds of a stay.
Keeps room inventory truthful across every channel the house sells on.
Moves luggage and cars without a guest ever watching it happen.
Runs the pass; tastes every sauce before it leaves the kitchen.
Keeps the cellar honest and pours what the dish actually needs.
Owns the section — timing, temperature, and the guest's pace.
Gets hot food to the eleventh floor still hot.
Bakes to the morning covers, never to the freezer.
Books the floor and fixes a service before it becomes a complaint.
Stock, licence and last orders; counts the cellar every Monday.
Inspects every east-wing room twice before it is released for sale.
Inspects every west-wing room twice before it is released for sale.
Turns the house linen daily and pulls anything past its life.
Restocks and reconciles every minibar before the folio closes.
Counts stock in and out so the contract crew never leaves short.
Turns down every occupied room between six and eight.
Keeps three cars running and every LOLER certificate current.
Holds each floor within two degrees, and the plant room within one.
The only pair of hands in the building between midnight and six.
Tests chemistry four times a day and logs every correction.
Owns the boards, the testing schedule and every emergency light.
Makes damage disappear before the room goes back on sale.
Covers every booked treatment; a cancellation is the last resort.
Watches the water and closes it the moment chemistry drifts.
data.note: No boot in 12 days — dark agent

data.desc: Checks every machine daily and signs the equipment log.
Sequences and re-sequences the day without a guest ever waiting.
Owns the protocol book and trains every therapist against it.
Carries one couple from first enquiry to the last dance.
Tests every microphone, screen and feed before the room fills.
Sets, serves and clears four hundred covers to the minute.
Converts enquiries without promising a room the house cannot set.
Cuts, conditions and places every arrangement the morning of.
Turns a ballroom around between two functions in a single night.
Patrols the perimeter hourly and logs every door opened out of hours.
Watches the estate and retains footage exactly as long as the law allows.
Issues, tracks and reconciles every key in the building.
First and last face at the entrance; refuses politely.
Books deliveries in, checks them off, and keeps the bay clear.
Buys to spec and to contract; challenges every off-contract order.
Matches invoice to order to receipt before a penny leaves the house.
The 03:00 run — reconciles every ledger while the house sleeps. Crons live in the graph like anyone else.
Chases every account before it ages past terms.
Counts what each department spends against what it was given.
Pays the house correctly and on time, every time.
Publishes every rota ahead of the week and resolves clashes before it goes out.
Owns induction and the annual refresh for every role in the house.
Fills a vacancy before the gap ever reaches the rota.
Runs every return-to-work conversation and knows who is struggling.
Reconciles hours worked to hours paid, weekly.
